title: Guyver and Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Guyver and Evangelion             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Jul 17, 2014 7:55 pm Me and my brother were talking and he mentioned Guvyer, he said Guyver Unit 1. I was just like, wait a second. Unit 1, that sound very familiar. We then started to make connections between Guyver and Evangelion, its definite that Evangelion has taken various influences. I'll begin, in Guyver they refer to the Guyvers as Guyver Units 1-3. In Evangelion they do the same, there is more: Both have cores, visually similar Both go berserk/out of control (In Eva they also flicker out of control on screen which is probably a reference) Pilot takes a backseat and has no control A shady organisation uncovering the past and their origins, seeded by aliens although for different purpose, similar to Nerv and Seele Gendo like character called Gyuo Symbiotic relationship with user Looks humanoid encased in armor Guyver Unit 1, Eva Unit 1 See the connections, im unsure as there may be more. It also helps if you have saw Guyver, its awesome so what do you think. http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bio_Booster_Armor_Guyver

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu Jul 17, 2014 11:02 pm ^ Honestly I'm surprised someone else noticed too, I'm also surprised someone else knows what Guyver is. The one thing most people remember is the movie with Mark Hamill in it.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jul 18, 2014 6:06 am What did you pick up on between Guyver and Evangelion yourself, I also wouldn't say that few know about Guyver. They released 2 anime TV shows and the OVA in English, also just to clarify most people remember the 2nd live action than the first because it has David Hayter in it. Aerial morph! Anyway do you guys think Evangelion did take influences from Guyver or not, I'd like to know.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Jul 18, 2014 11:59 am ^ Here's the only things I picked up on which you stated The cores Looks humanoid encased in armor Symbiotic relationship with user Oh and this one is silly but, both Units have pointy objects on their heads. Clearly one has a fin and the other a horn but they still have helmet adornment.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jul 18, 2014 3:37 pm Not even the berserk thing, it's clearly notable that Evangelion got that from Guyver. Then even flash out of control in Eva which is a term used for the Guyver. Also Guyver Unit 1, Eva Unit 1. Taking into account both of these and others we can make an assumption.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 18, 2014 6:13 pm See the wiki at http://wiki.evageeks.org/Geektionary#-gouki -号機 The Japanese way of saying “Unit # <so>”, usually in reference to pieces of machinery and other artificial constructs (like the Pokémon Porygon).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jul 19, 2014 8:48 am Cores aren't anything new either. That goes as far back as Mazinger, and they even submerge the pilot in liquid. The suits aren't new either, Bubblegum Crisis even had an (enemy IIRC) exo-suit that was sleek, purple, and had a single horn on its forehead. Not saying that there aren't similarities. It's just a lot harder to say "this is where Eva got this from". And I've seen Guyver, too. Nothing really rang as "Evangelion" at all to me, but maybe I'm just jaded from watching so much anime.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 19, 2014 9:02 am Evangelion comes from everything, and yet was still so original that pretty much everything in the late 90’s came from Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Jul 19, 2014 7:09 pm Yea it has a ton of influences and it would be kinda hard to pinpoint exactly which animes they all come from but I do think they took influence from Guyver. Units, cores, berserk and flashing out of control makes it kinda obvious as a Guyver reference of sorts. Which Guyver did you watch, I hope it was the ADV released one. The others are very bad and would be hard to make Eva connection from them.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 21, 2014 7:34 pm Out of Control, Bio-Booster and Bio-Booster II. They were all pretty bad IMO. And like it was mentioned earlier, "Gouki" is the name you give to equipment, it's common, the best word for it in English just happens to be "Unit".

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Jul 24, 2014 7:32 am I think I'll have to gravity bullet your ass after saying that, or get Aptom to bio merge with you. He's awesome by the way, I also wouldn't say any of the adaptations were bad so to speak. The original 2 are in terms of adapting the story of Guvyer but they are mindless fun, it also helps if your a fan of Guyver as that allows you to appreciate them better. Nobody can say the 2005 anime was bad even if you aren't a fan, that was actually a really good anime and adaption. Anyway I understand the gouki roughly translated as Unit, still think its a reference though because keyword "Out of Control". Meh, maybe its just me and my Guyverness taking over.

I'll begin, in Guyver they refer to the Guyvers as Guyver Units 1-3. In Evangelion
